title: A completed lookup with zero records is a valid answer — read the result body, not just status
summary: A registry lookup can complete cleanly (status:completed, HTTP 200) and carry no company — usually the correct answer for an out-of-coverage company, not an error. Empty shapes by mode: search -> success:true, results:[], total_results:0; lookup -> success:false, error:'Company not found' (or 'Unsupported country: XX'); vat -> valid:false (invalid) or valid:false+error (couldn't validate). Don't retry an out-of-coverage company (same empty, wasted call) — fix the input (real ID, right country, EL/XI VAT prefix). Exceptions worth ONE retry: a search empty (could be a swallowed upstream API error) and a vat valid:false+error (transient VIES). Empty writes no IDAP company_registry row; vat never writes one. Tell the user 'not in a public registry we can read', not 'failed'.
references: windmill-scripts/spidercompanydata_search.py:284-291 — search success:true + results[]/total_results even when empty
app/jobs/crm_sync_worker.py:1166-1199 — record needs name+source_id, else skipped (no IDAP row)
